channel.title: NYT > Movies
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Michael’ Review: A Jackson Biopic Leaves Too Much Unsaid
item.description: A biographical film produced by the estate of Michael Jackson flattens its subject to scrub his reputation.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: 4 Takeaways From ‘Lorne,’ the Movie About Lorne Michaels and ‘S.N.L.’
item.description: The documentary offers a rare behind-the-scenes look at a producer even his cast members say is hard to know.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik
item.media:description: Lorne Michaels in a scene from the new documentary about him.

item.title: ‘Funny Pages,’ ‘BlackBerry’ and More Streaming Gems
item.description: This month’s roundup of under-the-radar titles on your streaming subscription services features a host of memorable comedies, plus a harrowing documentary about the life of a war correspondent.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey

item.title: ‘The Interview’: Violence Shaped Charlize Theron. It Doesn’t Define Her.
item.description: The Oscar-winning actress on pain, healing and becoming an action hero.
item.dc:creator: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
item.media:credit: Devin Oktar Yalkin for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Reefer Madness,’ the P.S.A. That Backfired Spectacularly
item.description: The comically self-serious and outrageous 1936 morality tale, which warned the public about marijuana, became an unintentional parody and midnight-movie classic decades later.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: To Play a Man With Tourette’s, He Swapped the Script for Real Life
item.description: Robert Aramayo immersed himself in John Davidson’s life to prepare for the movie “I Swear.” It earned Aramayo a BAFTA, but Davidson’s tics became the talking point.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: British Academy of Film and Television
item.media:credit: Damien Maloney for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Mother Mary’ Review: Anne Hathaway Strikes a Pose
item.description: The actress plays a pop star who reunites with Michaela Coel’s fashion designer. But the spectacle you see onscreen is far more engaging than the dialogue.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Is the Movie Star Back? Sort Of.
item.description: Box office earnings seem no longer won by name alone. From “Project Hail Mary” to “F1,” celebrities are campaigning overtime on screens large and small to lead to hits.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: ‘Everyone Is Lying to You for Money’ Digs Into Cryptocurrency
item.description: “Everyone Is Lying to You for Money,” directed by Ben McKenzie, gives a solid introduction to virtual currency, and its traps.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson
item.category: Everyone Is Lying to You for Money (Movie)

item.title: ‘Beaches’ Review: A Classic Weepie Dries Its Tears
item.description: A new musical version of the 1980s tear-jerker comes to Broadway, but the production is too muddled to make an emotional impact.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes
item.media:credit: Rachel Papo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kelli Barrett, left, and Jessica Vosk as the besties Bertie and Cee Cee in “Beaches” at the Majestic Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Joy Harmon, Car-Washing Temptress in ‘Cool Hand Luke,’ Dies at 87
item.description: All it took was a bucket of soapy water and a slinky housedress for her to achieve movie immortality in the 1967 Paul Newman classic.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: Nathalie Baye, Versatile and Admired French Actress, Dies at 77
item.description: In films like “Day for Night,” “La Balance” and “The Return of Martin Guerre,” she was adept at portraying sensual, reflective or excitable characters.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.media:description: Nathalie Baye in 1986. President Emmanuel Macron of France saluted the “singular path she took in our French cinema — by the side of great directors, never abandoning a popular touch.”

item.title: Adrien Brody, Tessa Thompson and the Truth Behind ‘The Fear of 13’
item.description: “The Fear of 13,” about a man who spent two decades on death row for a murder he didn’t commit, is a story that the playwright says she “couldn’t shake.”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyall and Thea Traff
item.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: How ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Red Carpet Looks Came Together
item.description: The press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2” has been a style spectacle for its stars and their stylists, who have relied on group chats, sweatpants and camaraderie to pull it off.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: ‘Half Man,’ and 5 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: This new series, starring, written and directed by Richard Gadd of “Baby Reindeer,” comes to HBO, and Hulu observes 420 with a cavalcade of cannabis series.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:description: From left: Jamie Bell and Richard Gadd in “Half Man.”

item.title: Review: Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara Seduce in ‘Fallen Angels’
item.description: Lust is the comic engine driving the action of a riotous revival of one of Noël Coward’s early plays, with Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara, at Todd Haimes Theater.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

item.title: ‘All the President’s Men’ at 50: Times Journalists Look Back
item.description: New York Times staff members shared why they regard the 1976 film as one of the best movies about their profession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Vinnie Neuberg/The New York Times; Warner Brothers Pictures

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel in a Spooky, Tangled Thriller
item.description: Our film critic Alissa Wilkinson reviews “Mother Mary.”
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson and Edward Vega

item.title: Flowery Films Whose Plant Life Lingers in the Mind
item.description: Ten artists and designers reflect on the onscreen flora, from fantastical topiaries to scene-setting bouquets, that’s inspired them.
item.dc:creator: Laura Regensdorf

item.title: Lawsuit Against Alec Baldwin for ‘Rust’ Shooting Heads Toward Trial
item.description: Although the criminal case against Mr. Baldwin was thrown out, the actor still faces civil cases about the fatal shooting of a cinematographer in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: A judge dismissed a criminal case against Alec Baldwin in 2024 after determining that the prosecution had withheld evidence.

item.title: ‘Thrash’ and Other Action Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks include killer sharks, unlikely gangsters, and defiant ballerinas.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: 9 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Five Free Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks make up a quintet of paranoia that speaks to the ethos of unease and dread endemic to the modern moment
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu
item.media:credit: Columbia Pictures/Filmways Pictures, via Getty Images
item.media:description: John Travolta as a movie sound-effects technician who might have recorded a murder in “Blow Out,” directed by Brian De Palma.

item.title: ‘Lee Cronin’s The Mummy’ Is Out. Wait, Who’s Lee Cronin?
item.description: The latest take on Universal’s “Mummy” franchise has a director you may not know. We explain who he is, why his name is in the movie title and what he brings to the “Mummy” world.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Aguilar
item.media:description: Veronica Falcón in a scene from “Lee Cronin’s The Mummy.”

item.title: ‘Proof’ Review: Ayo Edebiri as a Math Girl, Interrupted
item.description: The actress stars as a haunted genius opposite Don Cheadle as her father in David Auburn’s 2001 drama. This revival, though, exposes the play’s lack of rigor.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.media:description: Don Cheadle, left, and Ayo Edebiri as father and daughter in the play “Proof” at the Booth Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: ‘Lee Cronin’s The Mummy’ Review: Fresh From the Sarcophagus
item.description: The movie revives one of cinema’s unforgettable monsters with a macabre makeover, but it spins out in the attempt.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold

item.title: ‘Normal’ Review: This Town Is Anything But
item.description: Bob Odenkirk plays a sheriff who uncovers a dangerous secret in this hyper-violent, small-town crime caper.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis

item.title: ‘Mad Bills to Pay’ Review: Growing Up Unexpectedly
item.description: A girlfriend’s pregnancy upends the life of a young man in the Bronx in this first feature by Joel Alfonso Vargas that unspools with sedulous care.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: ‘Erupcja’ Review: Dancing Through the Ash
item.description: Charli XCX stars in this drama about a young woman who can’t quite tell the difference between freedom and fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: ‘Eagles of the Republic’ Review: How Authoritarians Clip Wings
item.description: In the director Tarik Saleh’s latest feature on contemporary Egypt, a movie star is made to appear in a propaganda film.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: ‘Blue Heron’ Review: Rewinding Time to Find a Brother
item.description: Sophy Romvari’s superb debut feature blends memory, documentary and fiction to process a family wound.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: ‘Amrum’ Review: A Moral Awakening
item.description: In this World War II-era coming-of-age drama, a young boy living on a remote German island questions his parents for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Beatrice Loayza

item.title: ‘Balls Up’ Review: Offend It Like Beckham
item.description: Two condom salesmen, Mark Wahlberg and Paul Walter Hauser, embark on a bawdy, digressive picaresque in Peter Farrelly’s defiantly lowbrow film.
item.dc:creator: Calum Marsh
